text: This chapter introduces induction , a proof method which applies to the natural numbers and to other discrete types such as integers or pairs of natural numbers. We also introduce recursion , a method for defining sequences (and, more generally, functions from discrete types); induction is the canonical method for proving results about recursively-defined objects.
html: This chapter introduces induction, a proof method which applies to the natural numbers and to other discrete types such as integers or pairs of natural numbers. We also introduce recursion, a method for defining sequences (and, more generally, functions from discrete types); induction is the canonical method for proving results about recursively-defined objects.
zh: 本章介绍归纳法，这是一种适用于自然数以及整数、自然数对等其他离散类型的证明方法。我们还会介绍递归，这是一种定义数列（更一般地，定义从离散类型出发的函数）的方法；而归纳法正是证明关于递归定义对象的结果的标准方法。

text: In Section 6.1 - Section 6.3 , we use only the most traditional form of induction, proving a result for a natural number by relating it to the result for the previous natural number, and small variants on this form of induction. In Section 6.4 - Section 6.7 , we introduce strong induction , and, even more generally, well-founded induction . These induction principles are more flexible.
html: In Section 6.1 - Section 6.3, we use only the most traditional form of induction, proving a result for a natural number by relating it to the result for the previous natural number, and small variants on this form of induction. In Section 6.4 - Section 6.7, we introduce strong induction, and, even more generally, well-founded induction. These induction principles are more flexible.
zh: 在第 6.1 节到第 6.3 节中，我们只使用最传统形式的归纳法：通过把关于一个自然数的结果同关于前一个自然数的结果联系起来，来证明该结果，并使用这种归纳法的若干小变体。在第 6.4 节到第 6.7 节中，我们介绍强归纳法，以及更一般的良基归纳法。这些归纳原理更加灵活。

text: Let \(n\) be a natural number. Then \(n\) is either even or odd.

text: Proof

zh: 证明

text: We prove this by induction on \(n\) .
html: We prove this by induction on \(n\).

text: The base case is to show that 0 is either even or odd. We will show that it is even. Indeed, \(0=2\cdot 0\) .
html: The base case is to show that 0 is either even or odd. We will show that it is even. Indeed, \(0=2\cdot 0\).

text: Suppose now that for some natural number \(k\) , it is true that \(k\) is either even or odd.

text: Case 1 ( \(k\) is even): Then there exists an integer \(x\) such that \(k=2x\) , and so \(k+1 = 2x+1\) , so \(k+1\) is odd.
html: Case 1 (\(k\) is even): Then there exists an integer \(x\) such that \(k=2x\), and so \(k+1 = 2x+1\), so \(k+1\) is odd.
zh: 情形 1（\(k\) 为偶数）：则存在整数 \(x\)，使得 \(k=2x\)，于是 \(k+1 = 2x+1\)，所以 \(k+1\) 为奇数。

text: Case 2 ( \(k\) is odd): Then there exists an integer \(x\) such that \(k=2x+1\) , and
html: Case 2 (\(k\) is odd): Then there exists an integer \(x\) such that \(k=2x+1\), and
zh: 情形 2（\(k\) 为奇数）：则存在整数 \(x\)，使得 \(k=2x+1\)，并且

text: so \(k+1\) is even.
html: so \(k+1\) is even.
zh: 所以 \(k+1\) 为偶数。

text: Theorem
html: Theorem
zh: 定理

text: Let \(a, b, d\) be integers, and suppose that \(a\equiv b \mod d\) . Let \(n\) be a natural number. Then \(a^n\equiv b ^ n \mod d\) .
html: Let \(a, b, d\) be integers, and suppose that \(a\equiv b \mod d\). Let \(n\) be a natural number. Then \(a^n\equiv b ^ n \mod d\).
zh: 设 \(a,b,d\) 为整数，并假设 \(a\equiv b \mod d\)。设 \(n\) 为自然数。则 \(a^n\equiv b ^ n \mod d\)。

text: This is the power rule for modular arithmetic, the lemma Int.modEq.pow , which we stated without proof in Example 3.3.9 . It is one of the lemmas which are bundled together to form the tactic rel ’s capability for modular arithmetic.
html: This is the power rule for modular arithmetic, the lemma Int.modEq.pow, which we stated without proof in Example 3.3.9. It is one of the lemmas which are bundled together to form the tactic rel’s capability for modular arithmetic.
zh: 这是模算术的幂规则，即引理 Int.modEq.pow，我们在例 3.3.9 中曾不加证明地陈述过它。它是组合在一起形成 rel 策略模算术能力的引理之一。

text: We first note that \(a^0-b^0 = d\cdot 0\) , so \(d\mid a^0-b^0\) , so \(a^0\equiv b ^ 0 \mod d\) . This is the base case.
html: We first note that \(a^0-b^0 = d\cdot 0\), so \(d\mid a^0-b^0\), so \(a^0\equiv b ^ 0 \mod d\). This is the base case.

text: so \(d\mid a^{k+1}-b^{k+1}\) , so \(a^{k+1}\equiv b ^ {k+1} \mod d\) .

text: We have been encountering increasingly complicated induction principles, starting from “simple induction” in Example 6.1.1 and eventually reaching rather niche induction principles like “two-step induction from a specified starting point” in Example 6.3.5 . Rather than develop more and more exotic induction principles as our problems become yet more complicated, let’s explain a more general method: strong induction . This method lets us prove a proposition for the natural numbers one by one, relying at each step not just on the immediately previous step, but on any previous step.

This method lets us prove a proposition for the natural numbers one by one, relying at each step not just on the immediately previous step, but on any previous step.

text: Theorem

text: For any integers \(n\) and \(d\) , we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d) + d \cdot \operatorname{div}(n, d) = n\) .

text: We prove this by strong induction relative to the expression \(2n - d\) . Suppose that for all integers \(m\) and \(c\) with \(|2m - c|<|2n-d|\) , it is true that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d) + d \cdot \operatorname{div}(n, d) = n\) .
html: We prove this by strong induction relative to the expression \(2n - d\). Suppose that for all integers \(m\) and \(c\) with \(|2m - c|<|2n-d|\), it is true that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d) + d \cdot \operatorname{div}(n, d) = n\).
zh: 我们相对于表达式 \(2n-d\) 作强归纳证明。假设对所有满足 \(|2m-c|<|2n-d|\) 的整数 \(m\) 和 \(c\)，都有 \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c) + c \cdot \operatorname{div}(m, c) = m\)。

text: Theorem

zh: 定理

text: For any integers \(n\) and \(d\) , with \(d\) positive, we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\) is nonnegative.
html: For any integers \(n\) and \(d\), with \(d\) positive, we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\) is nonnegative.
zh: 对任意整数 \(n\) 和 \(d\)，若 \(d\) 为正，则 \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\) 非负。

text: Proof
html: Proof
zh: 证明

text: We prove this by strong induction relative to the expression \(2n - d\) . Suppose that for all integers \(m\) and \(c\) with \(c\) positive and \(|2m - c|<|2n-d|\) , it is true that \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c)\) is nonnegative.
html: We prove this by strong induction relative to the expression \(2n - d\). Suppose that for all integers \(m\) and \(c\) with \(c\) positive and \(|2m - c|<|2n-d|\), it is true that \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c)\) is nonnegative.
zh: 我们相对于表达式 \(2n-d\) 作强归纳证明。假设对所有整数 \(m\) 和 \(c\)，若 \(c\) 为正且 \(|2m-c|<|2n-d|\)，则 \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c)\) 非负。

text: Case 1 ( \(nd<0\) ): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n + d, d)\geq 0\) .
html: Case 1 (\(nd<0\)): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n + d, d)\geq 0\).
zh: 情形 1（\(nd<0\)）：由归纳假设，\(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n + d, d)\geq 0\)。

text: Case 2 ( \(0<d(n-d)\) ): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n - d, d)\geq 0\) .
html: Case 2 (\(0<d(n-d)\)): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n - d, d)\geq 0\).
zh: 情形 2（\(0<d(n-d)\)）：由归纳假设，\(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n - d, d)\geq 0\)。

text: Case 3 ( \(n=d\) ): Then \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)= 0\) , so \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\geq 0\) .
html: Case 3 (\(n=d\)): Then \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)= 0\), so \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\geq 0\).
zh: 情形 3（\(n=d\)）：\(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)= 0\)，所以 \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)\geq 0\)。

text: Case 4 ( \(0\le nd\le d^2\) and \(n\ne d\) ): Then since \(0\le nd\) and by hypothesis \(0<d\) , we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=n\geq 0\) .
html: Case 4 (\(0\le nd\le d^2\) and \(n\ne d\)): Then since \(0\le nd\) and by hypothesis \(0<d\), we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=n\geq 0\).
zh: 情形 4（\(0\le nd\le d^2\) 且 \(n\ne d\)）：由于 \(0\le nd\) 且根据假设 \(0<d\)，可得 \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=n\geq 0\)。

text: For any integers \(n\) and \(d\) , with \(d\) positive, we have that \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)<d\) .

html: We prove this by strong induction relative to the expression \(2n - d\). Suppose that for all integers \(m\) and \(c\) with \(c\) positive and \(|2m - c|<|2n-d|\), it is true that \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c)<c\).
zh: 我们相对于表达式 \(2n-d\) 作强归纳证明。假设对所有整数 \(m\) 和 \(c\)，若 \(c\) 为正且 \(|2m-c|<|2n-d|\)，则 \(\operatorname{mod}(m, c)<c\)。

text: Case 1 ( \(nd<0\) ): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n + d, d)<d\) .
html: Case 1 (\(nd<0\)): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n + d, d)<d\).

text: Case 2 ( \(0<d(n-d)\) ): Then by the inductive hypothesis \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)=\operatorname{mod}(n - d, d)<d\) .

text: Case 3 ( \(n=d\) ): Then \(\operatorname{mod}(n, d)= 0<d\) by hypothesis.

text: Proof
html: Proof

text: We prove this by strong induction on \(b\) . Suppose that for all integers \(x\) and \(y\) with \(|y|<|b|\) , it is true that \(0 \le \operatorname{gcd}(x, y)\) .
html: We prove this by strong induction on \(b\). Suppose that for all integers \(x\) and \(y\) with \(|y|<|b|\), it is true that \(0 \le \operatorname{gcd}(x, y)\).

text: Case 1 ( \(0<b\) ): Then \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) is equal to \(\operatorname{gcd}(b,\operatorname{mod}(a,b))\) which by the inductive hypothesis is nonnegative.

text: Case 2 ( \(b<0\) ): Then \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) is equal to \(\operatorname{gcd}(b,\operatorname{mod}(a,-b))\) which by the inductive hypothesis is nonnegative.

text: Case 3 ( \(b=0\) , \(0\le a\) ): Then we have that \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)=a\geq 0\) .
html: Case 3 (\(b=0\), \(0\le a\)): Then we have that \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)=a\geq 0\).

text: Prove that for all natural numbers \(a\) and \(b\) , \(P_{a,b} =P_{b,a}\) . theorem pascal_symm ( m n : ℕ ) : pascal m n = pascal n m := by match m , n with | 0 , 0 => sorry | a + 1 , 0 => sorry | 0 , b + 1 => sorry | a + 1 , b + 1 => sorry termination_by _ a b => a + b
html:
• Prove that for all natural numbers \(a\) and \(b\), \(P_{a,b} =P_{b,a}\).

    theorem pascal_symm (m n : ℕ) : pascal m n = pascal n m := by
  match m, n with
  | 0, 0 => sorry
  | a + 1, 0 => sorry
  | 0, b + 1 => sorry
  | a + 1, b + 1 => sorry
termination_by _ a b => a + b
zh: 证明：对所有自然数 \(a\) 和 \(b\)，都有 \(P_{a,b} =P_{b,a}\)。

text: Consider the function \(T\) defined recursively on the integers by, \[\begin{split}T(n)= \begin{cases} T(1-n)+2n-1,&0< n\\ T(-n),&n< 0\\ 0&n=0. \end{cases}\end{split}\] This recursive definition is well-founded, since its self-references are strictly decreasing in \(|3n-1|\) . Prove that for all integers \(n\) , \(T(n)=n^2\) . def T ( n : ℤ ) : ℤ := if 0 < n then T ( 1 - n ) + 2 * n - 1 else if 0 < - n then T ( - n ) else 0 termination_by T n => 3 * n - 1 theorem T_eq ( n : ℤ ) : T n = n ^ 2 := by sorry
html:
• Consider the function \(T\) defined recursively on the integers by,

    \[\begin{split}T(n)= \begin{cases} T(1-n)+2n-1,&0< n\\ T(-n),&n< 0\\ 0&n=0. \end{cases}\end{split}\]

    This recursive definition is well-founded, since its self-references are strictly decreasing in \(|3n-1|\).

    Prove that for all integers \(n\), \(T(n)=n^2\).

    def T (n : ℤ) : ℤ :=
  if 0 < n then
    T (1 - n) + 2 * n - 1
  else if 0 < - n then
    T (-n)
  else
    0
termination_by T n => 3 * n - 1

theorem T_eq (n : ℤ) : T n = n ^ 2 := by
  sorry
zh: 考虑递归定义在整数上的函数 \(T\)：\[\begin{split}T(n)= \begin{cases} T(1-n)+2n-1,&0< n\\ T(-n),&n< 0\\ 0&n=0. \end{cases}\end{split}\] 这个递归定义是良基的，因为它的自引用在 \(|3n-1|\) 上严格下降。证明：对所有整数 \(n\)，都有 \(T(n)=n^2\)。

text: Let \(a\) and \(b\) be integers, with \(b\) positive. Prove that there exists a unique integer \(r\) in the range \(0 \le r < b\) , such that \(a\equiv r\mod b\) . This theorem is an upgrade to Example 6.6.5 , stating uniqueness as well as existence. We stated it without proof in Example 4.3.4 (with the Lean name Int.existsUnique_modEq_lt ) and have been implicitly using it whenever we deduce a contradiction involving “obvious non-congruences”, such as in Example 4.4.3 . Suggested approach: Write the following as a stand-alone theorem uniqueness and prove it, somewhat along the lines of the special case proved in Example 4.3.4 : Let \(a\) and \(b\) be integers, with \(b\) positive. Let \(r\) and \(s\) be integers, both in the range \(0 \le r < b\) , \(0 \le s < b\) and both congruent to \(a\) modulo \(b\) . Show that they are equal. Then put all the pieces together, combining that with the argument from Example 6.6.5 . theorem uniqueness ( a b : ℤ ) ( h : 0 < b ) { r s : ℤ } ( hr : 0 ≤ r ∧ r < b ∧ a ≡ r [ ZMOD b ]) ( hs : 0 ≤ s ∧ s < b ∧ a ≡ s [ ZMOD b ]) : r = s := by sorry example ( a b : ℤ ) ( h : 0 < b ) : ∃! r : ℤ , 0 ≤ r ∧ r < b ∧ a ≡ r [ ZMOD b ] := by sorry
html:
• Let \(a\) and \(b\) be integers, with \(b\) positive. Prove that there exists a unique integer \(r\) in the range \(0 \le r < b\), such that \(a\equiv r\mod b\).

    This theorem is an upgrade to Example 6.6.5, stating uniqueness as well as existence. We stated it without proof in Example 4.3.4 (with the Lean name Int.existsUnique_modEq_lt) and have been implicitly using it whenever we deduce a contradiction involving “obvious non-congruences”, such as in Example 4.4.3.

    Suggested approach: Write the following as a stand-alone theorem uniqueness and prove it, somewhat along the lines of the special case proved in Example 4.3.4:

    Let \(a\) and \(b\) be integers, with \(b\) positive. Let \(r\) and \(s\) be integers, both in the range \(0 \le r < b\), \(0 \le s < b\) and both congruent to \(a\) modulo \(b\). Show that they are equal.

    Then put all the pieces together, combining that with the argument from Example 6.6.5.

    theorem uniqueness (a b : ℤ) (h : 0 < b) {r s : ℤ}
    (hr : 0 ≤ r ∧ r < b ∧ a ≡ r [ZMOD b])
    (hs : 0 ≤ s ∧ s < b ∧ a ≡ s [ZMOD b]) : r = s := by
  sorry

example (a b : ℤ) (h : 0 < b) : ∃! r : ℤ, 0 ≤ r ∧ r < b ∧ a ≡ r [ZMOD b] := by
  sorry
zh: 设 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 为整数，且 \(b\) 为正。证明：存在唯一整数 \(r\) 位于范围 \(0 \le r < b\) 内，使得 \(a\equiv r\mod b\)。这个定理升级了例 6.6.5：它不仅断言存在性，也断言唯一性。我们在例 4.3.4 中曾不加证明地陈述它（Lean 名称为 Int.existsUnique_modEq_lt），并且每当我们从“显然不成立的同余”推出矛盾时，例如例 4.4.3 中，都在隐式使用它。建议做法：把下面的内容写成一个独立定理 uniqueness 并证明它，思路大致仿照例 4.3.4 中证明的特殊情形：设 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 为整数，且 \(b\) 为正。设 \(r\) 和 \(s\) 为整数，二者都在范围 \(0 \le r < b\)、\(0 \le s < b\) 中，并且都与 \(a\) 模 \(b\) 同余。证明它们相等。然后结合例 6.6.5 中的论证，把各部分合在一起。

text: \(\underline{\text{If }0<b\text{ then }-b<\operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b}\) : By Example 6.6.3 and Example 6.6.4 , \(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b\) , which establishes the upper bound immediately and the lower bound since \[\begin{split}-b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b).\end{split}\]
html:
• \(\underline{\text{If }0<b\text{ then }-b<\operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b}\): By Example 6.6.3 and Example 6.6.4, \(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b\), which establishes the upper bound immediately and the lower bound since

    \[\begin{split}-b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b).\end{split}\]
zh: \(\underline{\text{若 }0<b\text{，则 }-b<\operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b}\)：由例 6.6.3 和例 6.6.4，\(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b)<b\)，这立即给出上界；下界则来自 \[\begin{split}-b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,b).\end{split}\]

text: \(\underline{\text{If }b<0\text{ then }b<\operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b}\) : We have that \(0<-b\) , so by Example 6.6.3 and Example 6.6.4 , \(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b\) , which establishes the upper bound immediately and the lower bound since \[\begin{split}b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b).\end{split}\]
html:
• \(\underline{\text{If }b<0\text{ then }b<\operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b}\): We have that \(0<-b\), so by Example 6.6.3 and Example 6.6.4, \(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b\), which establishes the upper bound immediately and the lower bound since

    \[\begin{split}b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b).\end{split}\]
zh: \(\underline{\text{若 }b<0\text{，则 }b<\operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b}\)：我们有 \(0<-b\)，所以由例 6.6.3 和例 6.6.4，\(0\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b)<-b\)，这立即给出上界；下界则来自 \[\begin{split}b&<0\\ &\le \operatorname{mod}(a,-b).\end{split}\]

text: Show that \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) is not just a common divisor of \(a\) and \(b\) (see Example 6.7.3 ), but their greatest common divisor : if \(d\) is an integer which divides both \(a\) and \(b\) , then it divides \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) . This problem does not need an induction; it is a direct corollary of Bézout’s identity ( Example 6.7.6 ). theorem gcd_maximal { d a b : ℤ } ( ha : d ∣ a ) ( hb : d ∣ b ) : d ∣ gcd a b := by sorry
html:
• Show that \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) is not just a common divisor of \(a\) and \(b\) (see Example 6.7.3), but their greatest common divisor: if \(d\) is an integer which divides both \(a\) and \(b\), then it divides \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\).

    This problem does not need an induction; it is a direct corollary of Bézout’s identity (Example 6.7.6).

    theorem gcd_maximal {d a b : ℤ} (ha : d ∣ a) (hb : d ∣ b) : d ∣ gcd a b := by
  sorry
zh: 证明 \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\) 不仅是 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 的公因子（见例 6.7.3），而且是它们的最大公因子：若整数 \(d\) 同时整除 \(a\) 和 \(b\)，则它整除 \(\operatorname{gcd}(a,b)\)。本题不需要归纳；它是贝祖等式（例 6.7.6）的直接推论。
